docs.title: Flow Reference
docs.text: This document covers every Flow-related control, helper, and node so you understand what is available and how it behaves.

docs.title: Live vs. Edit Mode
docs.text:
• Start / Stop Execution: The floating button in the action bar toggles between modes. Starting the flow animates edges, locks node movement, and runs the automation. Stopping saves the graph and returns you to edit mode.
  • Execution feedback: A stats card in the top-right shows execution time. A yellow banner appears if the connection temporarily drops.
  • Edge coloring: While live, connections show colors based on data type (booleans glow green/red, numbers show different colors). When stopped, edges turn grey.

docs.title: Canvas and Helpers
docs.text:
• Action toolbar: The vertical bar near the top-left contains workflow buttons: Start/Stop, Add Node, Devices, IO Table, Composites, Fit to View, Export, Import, Introduction. Some buttons are disabled while the flow is running.
  • Safe mode: The switch near the top-right shows warning messages when you try to make invalid connections (wrong data types or already-connected inputs). Turn it off to silence the warnings.
  • Helper lines: Displays alignment guides when dragging nodes near each other, helping keep your graph tidy.
  • Introduction dialog: Opens automatically on first use or when clicking the Introduction button to walk through the basics.

docs.title: Node Selection and Properties
docs.text:
• Add Node catalog: Click "Add Node" to browse available nodes. Search, filter by category tags, and drag nodes onto the canvas. Some nodes (like Axis nodes) open a setup dialog before placement.
  • Per-node drawer: Click a node in edit mode to open the side panel with Settings, Appearance, Inputs, and Outputs tabs. Each node also has a Help button for usage hints.
  • Deletion & composites: Select nodes and press Delete to remove them. When multiple connected nodes are selected, a "Create Composite" button appears to bundle them into a reusable group.

docs.title: Device and Composite Management
docs.text:
• Device dialog: Click "Devices" to add Axis devices with their connection details (IP, port, username, password, auth type, channel). You can test connections and manage device credentials here.
  • Composite manager: Click "Composites" to view, import, export, or delete saved composite nodes.

docs.title: Saving and Exporting
docs.text:
• Auto-save: The flow saves automatically when you stop execution.
  • Graph import/export: Export downloads your flow as a JSON file. Import loads a JSON file (only works when stopped). Users need the appropriate permission to see these buttons.
  • Logging: Add a Logging node to see runtime messages including errors, warnings, and info from your nodes.
  • IO Table: While running, click "IO Table" to see a live table of all node inputs/outputs and their current values.

docs.title: Node Setup Dialogs
docs.text:
Some nodes open a dialog before placement:

  • Axis Output: Select the device and which physical ports to control
  • Axis Metadata Subscribe: Choose the event topic and device
  • Metadata Event Create: A reminder appears that these events are only visible while the flow is running
  • JSON To Node: Enter sample JSON to generate output handles
  • Teleport: Pick which outputs to mirror from other nodes

docs.title: Node Library
docs.text: The sections below describe every node you can add, grouped by purpose.

docs.title: Axis Live Stream
docs.text:
Displays a live video stream from an Axis camera.

  • Inputs: Optional overlay data (ROI, Observations, Tripwire)
  • Outputs: None
  • Properties: Device selection, resolution, overlay settings

The stream retries automatically every 5 seconds if connection fails.

docs.title: Axis Device Log
docs.text:
Writes messages to the Axis device system log.

  • Inputs: Write (trigger), Message (text)
  • Outputs: None

docs.title: Axis SD Card Nodes
docs.text:
File operations on the camera's SD card:

Node Purpose Key Outputs SD Card Status Check card state Present, Writable, Full File Exists Check if file exists Exists Write File Create/overwrite file Written Append File Add to file Written Read File Read file contents Data, File Not Exists Remove File Delete file Removed List Files Get all files Data (JSON), Count Usage Storage used Used GB

docs.title: Axis Metadata Event (Subscribe)
docs.text:
Listens to Axis metadata events and exposes them as outputs.

  • Inputs: Enable
  • Outputs: Connected, Error, New Event, JSON, plus event-specific fields
  • Setup: Select the event topic through the dialog when adding the node

docs.title: Axis Metadata Event (Create)
docs.text:
Creates custom events visible to Axis rules while the flow runs.

  • Inputs: Send (trigger), plus custom fields you define
  • Outputs: Error
  • Properties: Event name, stateless/stateful mode, custom fields

Warning

Events are only visible to Axis rules while the flow is running.

docs.title: Axis Parameter
docs.text:
Reads any Axis parameter value.

  • Inputs: Parameter Name (e.g., Properties.System.SerialNumber)
  • Outputs: Value, Error

docs.title: Axis Outputs
docs.text:
Controls physical output ports (relays, digital outputs).

  • Inputs: Configured per device/port
  • Outputs: Success, Status, Error

Tip

Recreate the node if you change device credentials or port mappings.

docs.title: Vaxtor Cloud ANPR
docs.text:
Captures a camera snapshot and sends it to Vaxtor Cloud for plate recognition.

  • Inputs: Execute, Reset
  • Outputs: Success, Plate Found, plate details (Number, Country, State, Category, Confidence), vehicle details (Make, Model, Color, Class), Status, Error, Executed, Image ID

docs.title: Image Snapshot
docs.text:
Captures a JPEG snapshot from an Axis device.

  • Inputs: Capture, Reset
  • Outputs: Success, Image ID, Error, Executed

Use the Image ID with JPEG Viewer or HTTP nodes.

docs.title: JPEG Viewer
docs.text:
Displays a cached image on the canvas.

  • Inputs: Image ID (from Image Snapshot, HTTP, or Vaxtor)
  • Outputs: None
  • Properties: Width, height, border

docs.title: License Plates
docs.text:
Matches plates against the configured whitelist.

  • Inputs: Process, Plate, Reset
  • Outputs: Matched Plate, In List, Not In List, Expired, Not Valid Yet, Processed, Total Plates, plus tag outputs

docs.title: Enable Input
docs.text:
Always outputs true. Use as a permanent enable signal.

  • Outputs: True

docs.title: Button
docs.text:
Outputs true while pressed, false when released.

  • Outputs: Active

docs.title: In Time
docs.text:
Outputs true during configured daily time windows.

  • Outputs: In Time
  • Properties: Start/end times, allowed days

docs.title: Debounce
docs.text:
Filters noisy signals. Output changes only after input is stable for the set time.

  • Inputs: Input, Reset
  • Outputs: Value, Elapsed
  • Properties: Time amount and unit

docs.title: Edge Detection
docs.text:
Emits a pulse when input changes state.

  • Inputs: Value
  • Outputs: Value (pulse)
  • Properties: Rising or falling edge mode

docs.title: Pulse Buffer with Release
docs.text:
Stores pulses until released.

  • Inputs: Add, Release, Clear
  • Outputs: Pulse
  • Properties: Buffer size

docs.title: Next Cycle Pulse
docs.text:
Delays a pulse to the next execution cycle.

  • Inputs: Trigger
  • Outputs: Pulse

docs.title: One Shot Pulse After
docs.text:
Emits a delayed pulse after trigger.

  • Inputs: Trigger
  • Outputs: Pulse, Elapsed Time
  • Properties: Delay time and unit

docs.title: Timer On Delay (TON)
docs.text:
Output goes true after input stays true for the configured duration.

  • Inputs: Enable
  • Outputs: Active, Elapsed Time

docs.title: Timer Off Delay (TOF)
docs.text:
Goes true immediately, stays true for configured time after input goes false.

  • Inputs: Enable
  • Outputs: Active, Elapsed Time

docs.title: Pulse Timer (TP)
docs.text:
Fixed-length pulse on each rising edge.

  • Inputs: Enable
  • Outputs: Active, Elapsed Time

docs.title: Pulse Generator
docs.text:
Periodic pulses while enabled.

  • Inputs: Enable
  • Outputs: Active

docs.title: Step Sequencer
docs.text:
Advances through outputs on each trigger. Only one output is active at a time.

  • Inputs: Step
  • Outputs: Step 1 through Step N
  • Properties: Number of steps

docs.title: Timer Sequence
docs.text:
Runs through timed stages while enabled.

  • Inputs: Enable
  • Outputs: Elapsed Time, Timer Index, plus one output per timer stage
  • Properties: Duration and label for each stage

docs.title: Counter
docs.text:
Counts up/down on pulses.

  • Inputs: Up, Down, Reset
  • Outputs: Value

docs.title: Logic Gates
docs.text:
Standard boolean gates: AND, NAND, OR, NOR, XOR, XNOR, NOT.

  • Inputs: A, B (NOT uses only A)
  • Outputs: Result

docs.title: Compare
docs.text:
Compares two values.

  • Inputs: A, B
  • Outputs: Result
  • Properties: Operator (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=, contains, startswith, endswith), data type

docs.title: Value Selector
docs.text:
Routes one of two inputs based on a boolean.

  • Inputs: Select, Input 1, Input 2
  • Outputs: Value

docs.title: Multiplexer
docs.text:
Routes one of many inputs based on index.

  • Inputs: Index, Value-1 through Value-N
  • Outputs: Value, Selected Index

docs.title: Move
docs.text:
Passes value through when enabled.

  • Inputs: Enable, Value
  • Outputs: Value

docs.title: Math
docs.text:
Basic arithmetic: Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide.

  • Inputs: Enable, A, B
  • Outputs: Done, Result

docs.title: Advanced Math
docs.text:
Functions: ABS, SQRT, SIN, COS, TAN, SIGN.

  • Inputs: Enable, Value
  • Outputs: Done, Value

docs.title: Flip-Flop
docs.text:
Stores one bit with Set/Reset control.

  • Inputs: Set, Reset
  • Outputs: Value
  • Properties: RS or SR mode

docs.title: Value Latch
docs.text:
Stores a value until reset.

  • Inputs: Value, Set, Reset
  • Outputs: Value

docs.title: Edge Value Mux
docs.text:
On trigger, outputs a pulse and the corresponding value.

  • Inputs: Trigger 1-4, Value 1-4
  • Outputs: Pulse, Value

docs.title: Conversion Helpers
docs.text:
• Int to Bool: Non-zero becomes true
  • Bool to Int: True becomes 1, false becomes 0

docs.title: Queue FIFO
docs.text:
First-In First-Out buffer.

  • Inputs: Value, Add, Pop, Reset
  • Outputs: Value, Has, Count

docs.title: Queue LIFO
docs.text:
Last-In First-Out buffer (stack).

  • Same inputs/outputs as FIFO

docs.title: Data Table
docs.text:
Eight configurable registers.

  • Outputs: Eight values you can rename

docs.title: JSON Key Extractor
docs.text:
Extracts a value from JSON using a key path.

  • Inputs: Extract, String, Reset
  • Outputs: Value, Found
  • Properties: Key path (supports dot notation)

docs.title: JSON To Node
docs.text:
Splits a JSON object into individual outputs.

  • Inputs: Process, Reset, JSON
  • Outputs: Processed, Error, plus one output per key

docs.title: JSON Array Selector
docs.text:
Picks an element from a JSON array by index.

  • Inputs: Process, JSON Array
  • Outputs: Processed, Found, Error, Item JSON

docs.title: JSON Viewer
docs.text:
Displays formatted JSON.

  • Inputs: JSON
  • Properties: Indent, height, color theme

docs.title: SQL Nodes
docs.text:
• SQL Connection: Connect to MySQL, Postgres, SQL Server, or SQLite
  • SQL Query: Run SELECT and get results as JSON
  • SQL Insert/Update/Delete: Modify data

All SQL nodes share a connection key to keep the database connection alive.

docs.title: HTTP Request
docs.text:
Makes HTTP requests (GET, POST, etc.).

  • Inputs: Execute, Reset, Body
  • Outputs: Success, Status, Body, Error, Executed, Image ID
  • Properties: URL, method, headers, body, authentication, TLS options

docs.title: Webhook Listener
docs.text:
Runs an HTTP endpoint to receive external requests.

  • Inputs: Enable, Custom Response, Response Body, Response Code
  • Outputs: New Request, Body, Error, plus query parameter outputs
  • Properties: Port, path, authentication, rate limit, query parameters

docs.title: TCP Server
docs.text:
Listens on a TCP port.

  • Outputs: New Message, Message
  • Properties: Port, rate limit

docs.title: TCP Message Sender
docs.text:
Sends TCP messages.

  • Inputs: Send, Message
  • Outputs: Sent
  • Properties: Host, port

docs.title: Bool Debug Output
docs.text:
Shows boolean state with customizable icons and colors.

  • Inputs: Value
  • Properties: Icons, colors, labels for true/false states

docs.title: String Debug Output
docs.text:
Shows the current string value.

  • Inputs: Value

docs.title: Value Debug Output
docs.text:
Shows all input types in a table.

  • Inputs: Value - Bool, Value - Int, Value - Float, Value - String

docs.title: Logging
docs.text:
Shows runtime log messages with timestamps.

  • Inputs: None (automatically receives log messages)

docs.title: Oscilloscope
docs.text:
Plots up to five signals on a scrolling chart.

  • Inputs: Five signal inputs
  • Properties: Colors, min/max ranges

docs.title: Speedometer
docs.text:
Gauge display for numeric values.

  • Inputs: Value
  • Properties: Min, max, segments, colors

docs.title: Text
docs.text:
Static label on the canvas.

  • Properties: Text content, size, color

docs.title: Lua Script
docs.text:
Runs custom Lua code.

  • Inputs: Execute, plus additional inputs you can use in the script
  • Outputs: Error output, plus additional outputs you can set in the script

Built-in helpers: json_encode, json_decode, time(), date()

docs.title: Teleport
docs.text:
Mirrors signals without drawing long wires.

When adding, select which outputs to mirror. The teleport node then shows those values.

docs.title: Composite Nodes
docs.text:
Reusable node groups you create from selected nodes.

  • Create by selecting connected nodes and clicking "Create Composite"
  • Manage via the Composites button in the action bar
  • Import/export as JSON files

docs.title: Storage & SD Card Nodes
docs.text: Nodes for file operations on the Axis SD card and image caching.

docs.title: SD Card Overview
docs.text:
The Axis camera's SD card can be used for persistent storage of logs, configuration, and captured images. These nodes provide full file system access within the SD card.

Warning

Ensure an SD card is inserted and formatted before using these nodes. Check the camera's Storage settings.

docs.title: SD Card Status
docs.text:
Reports the current state of the SD card.

Category: Axis, SD-Card

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
Name Type Description Present Boolean True if card is inserted Writable Boolean True if card can be written to Full Boolean True if card is at capacity Setup Boolean True if card is being configured Exiting Boolean True if card is being ejected

Tip

Use this node to verify the SD card is available before attempting file operations.

docs.title: SD Card File Exists
docs.text:
Checks whether a file exists at the specified path.

Category: Axis, SD-Card

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge triggers the check Path String Relative path on SD card (e.g., logs/data.csv)

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Exists Boolean True if file exists

docs.title: SD Card Write File
docs.text:
Creates or overwrites a file with the provided content.

Category: Axis, SD-Card

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge triggers the write Path String Relative path on SD card Data String Content to write

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
Name Type Description Written Boolean Pulses true on success

Warning

This overwrites existing files completely. Use Append File for logging.

docs.title: SD Card Append File
docs.text:
Appends data to an existing file or creates a new one.

Category: Axis, SD-Card

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge triggers the append Path String Relative path on SD card Data String Content to append New Line After Boolean Add newline after data

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Written Boolean Pulses true on success

docs.title: Example: CSV Logging
docs.text:
1. Use CSV Row Builder to format data
  2. Connect to SD Card Append File with New Line After = true
  3. Set path to logs/events.csv

docs.title: SD Card Read File
docs.text:
Reads the contents of a file.

Category: Axis, SD-Card

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge triggers the read Path String Relative path on SD card Reset Boolean Clears cached content

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Data String File contents File Not Exists Boolean True if file not found

docs.title: SD Card Remove File
docs.text:
Deletes a file from the SD card.

Category: Axis, SD-Card

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge triggers deletion Path String Relative path to delete

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Removed Boolean Pulses true on success

docs.title: SD Card List Files
docs.text:
Returns a list of all files on the SD card.

Category: Axis, SD-Card

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge triggers the scan Reset Boolean Clears cached list

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Data String JSON array of file paths Count Integer Number of files

docs.title: Example Output
docs.text: ["logs/events.csv", "images/capture001.jpg", "config.json"]

docs.title: SD Card Usage
docs.text:
Reports storage consumption.

Category: Axis, SD-Card

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge triggers the query Reset Boolean Clears cached value

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Used GB Integer Gigabytes used on card

docs.title: Image Cache
docs.text: The image cache stores captured images in memory for quick access without writing to disk. This is useful for passing images between nodes (e.g., HTTP uploads, viewers) without large data transfers.

docs.title: Image Cache Info
docs.text:
Reports statistics about the in-memory image cache.

Category: Image, Cache

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
Name Type Description Has Images Boolean True if cache contains images Count Integer Number of cached images Total KB Float Total size in kilobytes

Note

The cache holds up to 10 images or 30 MB total. Oldest images are evicted when full.

docs.title: Image Cache Delete
docs.text:
Clears images from the cache.

Category: Image, Cache

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Clear Boolean Rising edge clears the cache

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Cleared Boolean Pulses true when complete Count Integer Number of images that were cleared

docs.title: Image Snapshot
docs.text:
Captures a JPEG snapshot from an Axis device.

Category: Axis, Image

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Capture Boolean Rising edge triggers capture Reset Boolean Clears the image reference

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Success Boolean True if capture succeeded Image ID String Reference ID for the cached image Error String Error message if failed Executed Boolean Pulses true when complete

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Device: Select from Device Management
  • Resolution: Optional resolution override

Tip

Pass the Image ID to JPEG Viewer or HTTP Request nodes instead of transferring raw image bytes.

docs.title: JPEG Viewer
docs.text:
Displays a cached image by its ID.

Category: Axis, Image

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Image ID String Reference from Image Snapshot, HTTP, or Vaxtor

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Width/Height: Display dimensions
  • Border: Show frame border

The viewer automatically fetches the image from the server-side cache, keeping websocket traffic minimal.

docs.title: Syslog
docs.text:
Writes messages to the Axis device system log.

Category: Axis, Debug

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Name Type Description Write Boolean Rising edge writes the message Message String Text to log

Tip

Use Syslog to record important events for troubleshooting via the camera's log viewer.

docs.title: String & Text Nodes
docs.text: Nodes for text manipulation, regular expressions, and encoding operations.

docs.title: String Concat
docs.text:
Joins two strings with an optional separator.

Category: String

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String A String First text String B String Second text Separator String Text between A and B (optional)

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Result String Combined text

docs.title: Example
docs.text: • A: Hello, B: World, Separator: → Result: Hello World

docs.title: String Replace
docs.text:
Replaces all occurrences of a substring.

Category: String

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Original text Old String Text to find New String Replacement text

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Result String Modified text

docs.title: String Split
docs.text:
Splits a string into parts at a separator.

Category: String

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Text to split Separator String Delimiter character(s)

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Parts JSON String JSON array of parts

docs.title: Example
docs.text: • String: a,b,c, Separator: , → Parts JSON: ["a","b","c"]

docs.title: String Join
docs.text:
Combines array elements into a single string.

Category: String

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Parts JSON String JSON array of strings Separator String Text between elements

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Combined text

docs.title: String Trim
docs.text:
Removes whitespace from both ends of text.

Category: String

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Text with possible whitespace

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Trimmed text

docs.title: String Lower
docs.text:
Converts text to lowercase.

Category: String

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Original text

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Lowercase text

docs.title: String Upper
docs.text:
Converts text to uppercase.

Category: String

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Original text

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Uppercase text

docs.title: String Empty
docs.text:
Checks if a string is empty or contains only whitespace.

Category: Logic, Compare

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Value String Text to check

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Result Boolean True if empty

docs.title: Regex Match
docs.text:
Tests if a pattern matches the input text.

Category: String, Regex

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Text to test Pattern String Regular expression

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Match Boolean True if pattern matches

docs.title: Example Patterns
docs.text: Pattern Matches ^[A-Z]{3} Starts with 3 uppercase letters \d{4} Contains 4 consecutive digits error\|warning Contains "error" or "warning"

docs.title: Regex Replace
docs.text:
Replaces text matching a pattern.

Category: String, Regex

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Original text Pattern String Regular expression to match Replacement String Replacement text

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
Name Type Description Result String Modified text

Tip

Use $1, $2 in the replacement to reference capture groups from the pattern.

docs.title: Regex Find All
docs.text:
Finds all matches of a pattern.

Category: String, Regex

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Text to search Pattern String Regular expression

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Matches JSON String JSON array of all matches

docs.title: Base64 Encode
docs.text:
Encodes text as Base64.

Category: Encoding, String

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Text to encode

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Base64 String Encoded text

docs.title: Base64 Decode
docs.text:
Decodes Base64 text.

Category: Encoding, String

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Base64 String Encoded text

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Decoded text

docs.title: String to Int
docs.text:
Parses a string as an integer.

Category: Transform, Data

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Numeric text (e.g., "42")

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Int Integer Parsed number (0 if invalid)

docs.title: String to Float
docs.text:
Parses a string as a floating-point number.

Category: Transform, Data

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Numeric text (e.g., "3.14")

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Float Float Parsed number (0.0 if invalid)

docs.title: Int to String
docs.text:
Converts an integer to text.

Category: Transform, Data

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Int Integer Number to convert

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Text representation

docs.title: Float to String
docs.text:
Converts a floating-point number to text.

Category: Transform, Data

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Float Float Number to convert

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description String String Text representation

docs.title: Int to Bool
docs.text:
Converts an integer to boolean.

Category: Transform, Logic

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Value Integer Number to convert

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Bool Boolean True if non-zero

docs.title: Bool to Int
docs.text:
Converts a boolean to integer.

Category: Transform, Logic

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Value Boolean Boolean to convert

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Int Integer 1 if true, 0 if false

docs.title: Date String
docs.text:
Formats the current date and time.

Category: Utility, Time

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge updates outputs

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Updated Boolean Pulses true when updated Text String Formatted date/time Unix (s) Integer Unix timestamp in seconds Unix (ms) Integer Unix timestamp in milliseconds

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Format: Date/time format string (e.g., 2006-01-02 15:04:05)

Note

Uses Go time format: 2006 = year, 01 = month, 02 = day, 15 = hour, 04 = minute, 05 = second.

docs.title: Random Number
docs.text:
Generates a random integer or float within a range.

Category: Utility

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge generates new value

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Updated Boolean Pulses true when generated Value Int/Float Random number

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Type: Integer or Float
  • Min: Minimum value (inclusive)
  • Max: Maximum value (inclusive)

docs.title: CSV Row Builder
docs.text:
Constructs a CSV row from dynamic inputs.

Category: Output, Transform

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Execute Boolean Rising edge builds the row Column inputs String One input per column

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Built Boolean Pulses true when complete Row String CSV-formatted row

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Columns: Define column names; each becomes an input
  • Separator: Field separator (default: ,)
  • Quote: Quote character for text fields

Tip

Combine with SD Card Append File to log data to CSV files.

docs.title: Timing Nodes
docs.text: Nodes for time-based logic, counting, and delay generation.

docs.title: Timer on Delay (TON)
docs.text:
The Timer on Delay outputs true only after the input has been continuously true for the configured duration.

Category: Time, Edge

docs.title: Functionality
docs.text: When Enable goes true, the timer starts counting. If Enable stays true for the full duration, Active becomes true. If Enable goes false before the time elapses, the timer resets.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Enable Boolean Starts the timer when true

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Active Boolean True after delay completes Elapsed Time Integer Milliseconds counted so far

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Amount: Time value
  • Unit: ms, seconds, or minutes

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Filter brief sensor triggers
  • Require sustained conditions before acting
  • Implement "hold to confirm" patterns

docs.title: Timer off Delay (TOF)
docs.text:
The Timer off Delay goes true immediately when enabled, then stays true for a configured time after the input goes false.

Category: Time, Edge

docs.title: Functionality
docs.text: When Enable goes true, Active immediately becomes true. When Enable goes false, the timer starts. Active remains true until the delay expires.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Enable Boolean Triggers the timer

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Active Boolean True during input and delay Elapsed Time Integer Milliseconds in delay phase

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Keep lights on after motion stops
  • Extend relay activation after trigger ends
  • Smooth intermittent signals

docs.title: Pulse Timer (TP)
docs.text:
The Pulse Timer emits a fixed-length pulse on each rising edge of the input.

Category: Time, Edge

docs.title: Functionality
docs.text: Each rising edge of Enable starts a pulse of the configured duration. The pulse length is fixed regardless of how long the input stays true.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Enable Boolean Rising edge triggers pulse

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Active Boolean True during pulse Elapsed Time Integer Milliseconds into pulse

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Generate consistent pulse widths
  • Trigger fixed-duration actions
  • Normalize varying input signals

docs.title: Pulse Generator
docs.text:
The Pulse Generator produces periodic single-cycle pulses while enabled.

Category: Time, Edge

docs.title: Functionality
docs.text: While Enable is true, outputs a one-cycle true pulse at regular intervals based on the configured period.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Enable Boolean Enables pulse generation

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Active Boolean Periodic pulse output

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Amount: Time between pulses
  • Unit: ms, seconds, or minutes

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Periodic polling triggers
  • Heartbeat signals
  • Timed sampling

docs.title: Timer Sequence
docs.text:
The Timer Sequence node activates a series of outputs one after another.

Category: Time, Edge

docs.title: Functionality
docs.text: When Enable is true, it cycles through configured timers. Each timer keeps its corresponding output high for its duration, then hands off to the next. When all timers complete, it wraps back to the first.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Enable Boolean Starts the sequence (false resets)

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Elapsed Time Integer Time in current slot (ms) Timer Index Integer Current active timer (0-based) Timer 1, Timer 2... Boolean Active during respective slot

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
1. Add Timer: Use Properties panel to add steps
  2. Duration: Set length and unit for each step
  3. Label: Rename outputs (e.g., "Red", "Yellow", "Green")

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Traffic light sequences
  • Multi-stage processes
  • Timed state machines

docs.title: One Shot Pulse After
docs.text:
The One Shot Pulse node generates a delayed pulse.

Category: Time, Edge

docs.title: Functionality
docs.text: On a rising edge at Trigger, waits for the configured delay, then emits a single-cycle true pulse.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Trigger Boolean Rising edge starts delay

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Pulse Boolean Delayed output pulse Elapsed Time Integer Time waited so far (ms)

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Delayed reactions
  • Scheduled triggers
  • Timeout implementations

docs.title: Debounce
docs.text:
The Debounce node filters noisy boolean signals.

Category: Logic, Time

docs.title: Functionality
docs.text: The output only changes when the input has been stable for the configured duration. This eliminates bouncing or flickering.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Input Boolean Signal to debounce Reset Boolean Clears the timer

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Value Boolean Debounced output Elapsed Integer Time input has been stable (ms)

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Amount: Stability time required
  • Unit: ms, seconds, or minutes

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Physical button inputs
  • Noisy sensor signals
  • Unstable network states

docs.title: Counter
docs.text:
The Counter node tracks a number based on input pulses.

Category: Memory, Edge

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Up Boolean Rising edge increments Down Boolean Rising edge decrements Reset Boolean Sets count to zero

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Value Integer Current count

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Event counting
  • Occupancy tracking
  • Cycle counting

docs.title: Step Sequencer
docs.text:
The Step Sequencer advances through outputs on each trigger pulse.

Category: Memory, Edge

docs.title: Functionality
docs.text: Each rising edge of Step advances to the next output. Only one output is true at a time. After the last step, wraps to the first.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Step Boolean Rising edge advances

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Step 1...N Boolean Current step indicator

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: • Steps: Number of outputs (2-16)

docs.title: Measure Time
docs.text:
The Measure Time node measures duration between events.

Category: Utility

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Start Boolean Rising edge starts timer Stop Boolean Rising edge stops timer Reset Boolean Clears the measurement

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description Duration ms Integer Measured time in milliseconds Running Boolean True while measuring Done Boolean Pulses true when stopped

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Process timing
  • Response time measurement
  • Performance monitoring

docs.title: In Time
docs.text:
The In Time node outputs true during a configured daily time window.

Category: Time

docs.title: Functionality
docs.text: Compares current time against a configured schedule. Outputs true when within the time range on allowed days.

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Name Type Description In Time Boolean True during configured window

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Start Time: Beginning of window (e.g., 08:00)
  • End Time: End of window (e.g., 17:00)
  • Days: Which days of week are active

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Business hours detection
  • Scheduled operations
  • Time-based access control
